parts:
Randia ulei is a plant species known to have potential medicinal properties. 
Randia ulei is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, where the roots, leaves, and bark are utilized for their medicinal value. 
The medicinal parts of the plant Randia ulei include its roots, leaves, and bark, which are rich in bioactive compounds. 
These bioactive compounds are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

intro: Randia ulei, also known as "São Tomé," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical rainforests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Randia ulei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
